description_long: This is a traditional barn structure with a rectangular footprint, clad in vertically arranged wooden boards and topped with a prominent gable roof made of corrugated metal. The barn sits on a stone foundation, giving it a sturdy and elevated appearance. The front features a large, double-leaf hinged door with visible hardware, a smaller access door to the left, and a single square window above the main entrance. The roof is slightly weathered, showing some signs of rust and age, while the wooden siding displays a rustic, timeworn texture. The sides of the barn are unadorned, emphasizing the vertical siding and the expanse of the metal roof. This type of barn is commonly found in rural or peri-urban settings but could also appear in urban parks or as a repurposed structure in city environments.
description: A traditional rectangular barn with vertical wood siding, a gable metal roof, and a large double door with a single window above. The structure sits on a stone foundation and has a rustic, weathered appearance.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long expanse of vertical wood siding and the full length of the metal gable roof, with no doors or windows visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the large double-leaf hinged barn door, a smaller access door to the left, and a single square window above, all framed by the gable roof.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with uninterrupted vertical wood siding and the metal roof, no openings visible.
description_view_3: Rear view identical to the front, featuring the same large double door, smaller access door, and single window above.
attribute_door_style: double-leaf hinged barn door with a smaller adjacent hinged access door
attribute_window_arrangement: single square window centered above the main door on the front and rear facades
